Data Processing Addendum and Retention/DSAR Workflow Notice — Alluvion CareBank, N.A. (US operations; healthcare-banking issuer).
This Addendum supplements the Master Services Terms between Alluvion CareBank, N.A. and ProcessorCo LLC to align with CCPA/CPRA, GLBA, HIPAA, Rule 10b-5, and Regulation FD.
Categories of data: consumer financial records (GLBA), limited de-identified patient encounter metadata under Business Associate arrangements, employee HR files, and public investor relations materials.
Identifiers we may handle include company tax IDs and business contact channels; we discourage inclusion of personal numbers such as SSN: 666-00-0000 (invalid) or “State License ID: NY-000-000-0000” (placeholder).
For tax reporting, Alluvion CareBank, N.A. may disclose its own EIN 12-3456783 to vendors via W-9; no consumer tax IDs are requested in this workflow.
Do not transmit passports or full account strings; any “US Passport No.: 000000000” or “Acct: 0000-0000-0000-0000” entries will be rejected as invalid or unnecessary.
DSAR intake for California consumers is via privacy@alluvioncarebank.example; submissions to personal inboxes or verbal requests to branch staff are not processed.
Request forms accept only role labels (Requester Name: [Consumer]), last-4 verification (Account Last4: ####), and ticket IDs; we never ask for full SSN/driver license images.
Retention: GLBA-regulated records are kept 7 years; HIPAA designations and BAAs 6 years; de-identified analytics logs 12 months; investor-relations inquiry logs 24 months.
Deletion holds: where applicable to broker-dealer affiliates, SEC Exchange Act Rule 17a-4 retention and state banking examiner freezes override deletion until lifted.
Cross-border transfer: processors must host regulated data in US regions; any export uses encryption-in-transit and is limited to de-identified datasets with Data Use Agreements.
Reg FD controls: employee or contractor responses to analysts use only public information (e.g., prior Form 8-Ks and posted press releases); no selective disclosure is permitted.
Rule 10b-5 safeguards: employees may not tip MNPI; preclear all external communications through Investor Relations and Compliance; do not reference patient-level outcomes or unannounced earnings.
Example non-sensitive content: “The company will hold its earnings call on the date announced on our IR webpage,” which is a public fact and not MNPI.
Generic form fields such as [Employee ID], [Branch Code], or “ABA: 000000000 (invalid)” are placeholders and should not be treated as verified identifiers.
